summary: Re-verified 4 polish items after Vite frontend restart. Major improvements from iteration 269: (1) OPERATOR 403s FULLY FIXED — 0 /api/operators calls fired across all 8 management pages for operator role; (2) ADMIN 200 flow FULLY WORKING — 16 /api/operators calls all returned 200 (both /operators/ and /operators/by-service?service_type=X); (3) Orders FR mostly landed — Filtres title, search placeholder ('Rechercher par numéro, service ou catégorie…'), stat cards (En attente / Terminée / Annulée) and results counter ('Affichage de 10 sur 278 commandes') all translated. Row action buttons 'View'/'Cancel' STILL show English (should be 'Voir'/'Annuler'); (4) Settings FR mostly landed — 'Informations de base', 'À remplir une fois · verrouillé après enregistrement', 'Nom complet', 'Adresse e-mail', 'Numéro de téléphone', 'Date de naissance', 'Numéro de CNI / Passeport', 'Adresse postale', 'Ville', 'Région / Département', 'Code postal', 'Pays' all correct. Gender label + gender options + '(locked)' suffix on Gender field STILL English; (5) EventTicket FR FULLY FIXED — right-panel description now reads 'Présentez ce billet (ou le QR ci-dessous) à l'entrée du lieu.' Approx 90% of the polish work is now correctly in place.

ui_bugs.issue: Order card row buttons 'View' and 'Cancel' still render in English even when i18n is FR. All other Orders strings translate correctly. Expected 'Voir' and 'Annuler'.

Gender label still reads 'Gender' (expected 'Genre'), the '(locked)' suffix on this field is still English (expected '(verrouillé)'), and the gender <select> options are still 'Male / Female / Other / Prefer not to say' (expected 'Homme / Femme / Autre / Préfère ne pas préciser'). Every other field in the Basic Information + Address blocks translates correctly.

operator_403_check.note: canListOperators() guard is now effective on ALL 8 pages. operator@test.com still lands on /admin/analytics after login, but no /operators/ requests fire from there or downstream.

found_fr: Rechercher par numéro, service ou catégorie… (placeholder)
not_verified_in_this_run: Catégorie/Statut/Trier — inside filter popover which was not opened

found_fr: À remplir une fois · verrouillé après enregistrement
still_english: Male / Female / Other / Prefer not to say (gender <select> options)

found_fr: Présentez ce billet (ou le QR ci-dessous) à l'entrée du lieu.
event_ticket_fr.note: doors_open variant not verified because the sample event ticket did not have doors_open set. Rules text ('No outside drinks', 'Photo ID required') is still English but that was out-of-scope for this iteration.

action_items: Orders.jsx: translate the row action buttons — the 'View' and 'Cancel' labels on each order card are still hard-coded English. Search for `>View<` and `>Cancel<` (or a helper renderer) around lines 721/731 as noted in the review — the t() keys look wrong or aren't wired to the rendered JSX.

Settings.jsx: translate the Gender field. (a) label 'Gender' → t('settings.profile.gender_label') = 'Genre', (b) '(locked)' suffix on this specific field → '(verrouillé)' (other fields already use this pattern), (c) the <select> <option> labels ('Male'/'Female'/'Other'/'Prefer not to say') → 'Homme'/'Femme'/'Autre'/'Préfère ne pas préciser'.
(Optional) Verify Category / Statut / Trier dropdown labels inside the Filters popover render translated — this run did not open the popover, so untested.
critical_code_review_comments: Orders.jsx has a mixture of translated strings and hard-coded English within the same card layout — this suggests the row-action button block was missed by the i18n sweep. Consider grepping for `>View<`, `>Cancel<`, `>Rate<` in the file to make sure no literal strings remain.

Settings.jsx Gender block is a distinct sibling from the other fields — it's the only one that uses a <select>. Whoever added the FR pass likely translated the surrounding <input> fields but skipped the <select> + its <option>s and the label. Standardise by wrapping options in a `genderOptions = useMemo(() => [{value:'male', label: t(...)}...])` pattern.
test_credentials: Operator: operator@test.com / testpassword123, Admin: admin@test.com / testpassword123, Customer: customer@test.com / testpassword123
context_for_next_testing_agent: The polish landed cleanly this time — just 2 residual leaks: (1) Orders row buttons 'View'/'Cancel' in EN and (2) Settings Gender label + gender <select> options + '(locked)' suffix on Gender in EN. Login flow: force `localStorage.setItem('oryno_language','fr')` BEFORE navigating to the login card, then click 'Se connecter à votre compte' → email → 'Continuer' → password → Enter. Session lives in httpOnly cookies so page.goto() with new context loses it — do everything in one script. The lang dropdown button is NOT visible on the customer chrome header (only 'Cameroon · Global' pill + user avatar are shown), so localStorage bootstrap is the only reliable trigger for FR before login.
rca of the issue: Operator 403 fix: canListOperators(user) guard is now present + effective in all 8 management pages (BanquetManagement:1314, CarRentalManagement:443, CinemaManagement:222, EventsManagement:117, HotelManagement:176, LaundryManagement:198, PackageManagement:336, RestaurantManagement:316). Confirmed via network capture — zero /api/operators requests fire for operator role. Prior iteration 269's suspicion of a shell-level unguarded fetch was WRONG — the fix simply hadn't propagated to the running Vite bundle. i18n misses: the remaining Orders/Settings leaks are just missed t() calls on specific JSX subtrees (row buttons block in Orders, Gender <select> block in Settings), not a systemic i18n plumbing issue — the same file/component has neighbouring strings that DO translate.
